Configurations
  • Terminal
  • View page source

Terminal

This section documents the terminal configuration.

Overview

The setup uses foot as the primary terminal emulator.

Configuration is defined in:

  • term/foot/foot.ini

This file is intended to be placed in:

  • ~/.config/foot/foot.ini

Configuration

foot.ini

Defines the behavior and appearance of the terminal, including:

  • Font configuration

  • Colors and theme

  • Window behavior

  • Scrolling and input settings

 1[main]
 2font=Red Hat Mono:style=Regular:size=13
 3dpi-aware=no
 4pad=8x8
 5
 6[cursor]
 7style=beam
 8blink=no
 9
10[mouse]
11hide-when-typing=yes
12
13[colors-dark]
14background=f5f5f5
15foreground=000000
16
17regular0=222222
18regular1=ee0000
19regular2=3a7d44
20regular3=b8860b
21regular4=1a6b8a
22regular5=8b3a8b
23regular6=1a7b7b
24regular7=dddddd
25
26bright0=666666
27bright1=ff3b30
28bright2=4e9e5a
29bright3=d4a017
30bright4=2196b0
31bright5=b050b0
32bright6=20a0a0
33bright7=f5f5f5
34
35[key-bindings]
36scrollback-up-page=Control+u
37scrollback-down-page=Control+d
38clipboard-copy=Control+Shift+c
39clipboard-paste=Control+Shift+v
40search-start=Control+Shift+r

Notes

  • The configuration is minimal and relies mostly on sensible defaults.

  • Font configuration may depend on system-installed fonts.

  • Behavior may interact with the window manager (e.g. floating, sizing).

See also:

  • Window Managers

  • Shells
